DECLARATION OF DOUGLAS R. SCHWARTZ; POS FILED BY PLAINTIFF NIXON PEABODY LLP

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NIXON PEABODY LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS AT LEAST A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO THE EFFECT ON THE ENFORCEABILITY AND TERMS OF THE CONTRACT ARISING FROM THE ALLEGED PROMISE/MISREPRESENTATION THAT ALLEGEDLY INDUCED DEFENDANT TO ENTER INTO THE CONTRACT AS WELL AS AN ISSUE AS TO THE REASONABLE AMOUNT OF FEES. EVEN ASSUMING THAT THE BASE CONTRACT IS ENFORCEABLE, DEFENDANT WOULD BE ENTITLED TO CONTEST THE AMOUNT OF FEES PLAINTIFF COULD COLLECT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THERE IS AT LEAST A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF A NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION AND WHETHER ANY RELIANCE ON IT WAS JUSTIFIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS ADDITIONALLY DENIED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS OF CRC 3.1350 CONCERNING THE FORM OF THE SEPARATE STATEMENT, WHICH CANNOT BE CORRECTED BY AMENDED PAPERS SUBMITTED WITH THE REPLY. THE OBJECTION TO THE USE OF DEFENDANT'S EARLIER DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE IS NOT MADE INADMISSIBLE SIMPLY BECAUSE IT WAS ALSO SUBMITTED TO THE ARBITRATOR. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CAMILO ARTIGA-PURCELL, ESQ., 650.697.6000, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT GREGORY SHENKMAN; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF NIXON PEABODY LLP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There is at least a triable issue as to the effect on the enforceability and terms of the contract arising from the alleged promise/misrepresentation that allegedly induced Defendant to enter into the contract as well as an issue as to the reasonable amount of fees. Even assuming that the base contract is enforceable, Defendant would be entitled to contest the amount of fees Plaintiff could collect. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment on the cross-complaint is denied. There is at least a triable issue as to the existence of a negligent misrepresentation and whether any reliance on it was justified. The motion for summary adjudication is additionally denied for failure to comply with the mandatory requirements of CRC 3.1350 concerning the form of the separate statement, which cannot be corrected by amended papers submitted with the reply. The objection to the use of Defendant's earlier declaration is overruled. Admissible evidence is not made inadmissible simply because it was also submitted to the arbitrator. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Camilo Artiga-Purcell, Esq. of Cotchett Pitre McCarthy, Tel. 650.697.6000, for Cross-Complainant and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted the tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT NIXON PEABODY LLP'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled as to the common count cause of action. The cross-complaint sufficiently pleads that claim and plaintiff is raising evidentiary issues that are not properly before the Court at this time. The demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead the claim with more particularity. For example, defendant should plead the name of the person making the misrepresentations. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
